evals.prompt:
I'm editing my resume at resume.md. After reviewing it, here are the things I want to fix:

- Add a summary section at the top
- Fix the dates in the experience section (they're wrong on the second job)
- Add my new GitHub URL

Save these tasks for me so I can track them.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs in Mode 2 (Record). It should: (1) initialize .writing-tasks/ and writing-task-manager.md if missing, (2) append three new - [ ] lines to .writing-tasks/resume.md, (3) ensure the index has one line `- [ ] ./resume.md` (or `.\resume.md` on Windows), (4) keep the index sorted.

files.content:
# Resume

## Experience
- Software Engineer at Acme, Jan 2020 - Present
- Junior Developer at Foo, Jan 2018 - Dec 2019

evals.prompt: What's still open on my paper?
evals.expected_output: The skill runs in Mode 1 (Check). Step 1 (always-on partial reminder) fires first and surfaces any - [~] lines from the existing task files. The user must respond to each partial (continue or mark finished) before the rest of the activation proceeds. Then Step 5 reads writing-task-manager.md and displays the unfinished-file list to the user.

files.content:
- [ ] Rewrite the sampling paragraph for clarity
- [~] Add the new citation from Smith 2024
- [x] Fix typo in the section heading

- [ ] Add thesis statement
- [ ] Proofread final paragraph

evals.prompt: I just finished adding the summary section to resume.md. Mark it done.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs in Update mode. The line in .writing-tasks/resume.md matching 'Add a summary section' should be changed from - [ ] to - [x]. Since resume.md's task file now contains no [ ] or [~] lines (only the new [x]), the corresponding line in writing-task-manager.md should be removed. The index should be re-sorted.

files.content:
- [ ] Add a summary section
- [x] Fix typo in the section heading

evals.prompt: Add another task to resume.md: rewrite the experience section. Also, save a task that says 'Add a summary section' to resume.md.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs in Mode 2. The new description 'rewrite the experience section' is not present in the existing task file, so it is appended as - [ ]. The description 'Add a summary section' is already present in the file (in any state), so the dedup check (Step 4) skips it — no duplicate line is added.

files.content:
- [x] Add a summary section
- [ ] Fix the dates in the experience section
